text:
Over the last 
more than 70 years, preventive, diagnostic and therapeutic services have been provided through 
this network managed by States.
label: SERVICE_GAP

text:
It has been observed that a big gap exists between Primary Health 
Centres (PHCs)/ Community Health Centres (CHCs) and tertiary care hospitals with state-of-the 
art-facilities created by the Centre and also by some of the State Governments.
label: INFRA_GAP

text:
7.2	
Further, wide variations exist in the pattern of diseases prevalent in different geographical 
areas and local conditions which require development of State/area specific and disease specific 
strategies to provide better health care facilities, thus ensuring that the modern technology is 
available to the general public.
label: INFRA_GAP

text:
Transfer of research findings/technologies at the rural level has 
been found to be major lacuna in providing quality medical services to the rural population.
label: ACCESS_GAP

text:
7.3	
To bridge the gap, Department of Health Research rolled out a central sector umbrella 
scheme for Development of Infrastructure for Health Research in 2013-14, i.e. during 12th Five 
Year Plan (2012-13 to 2016-17).
label: INFRA_GAP

text:
The umbrella scheme has 02 sub-schemes, namely (i) establishing 
Multidisciplinary Research Units (MRUs) in Government Medical Colleges and Research Institutes, 
and (ii) establishing Model Rural Health Research Units (MRHRUs) in rural areas under the 
mentorship of ICMR institutes.
label: SERVICE_GAP

text:
7.4	
The scheme was extended from 2017-18 to 2019-20 to be co-terminus with the 14th Finance 
Commission Period.
label: SERVICE_GAP

text:
Subsequently, Department of Expenditure vide its Notification No. 42(02)/PF-
II/2014 dated 10.01.2020 approved an interim extension to all ongoing schemes till 31.03.2021 or 
till the date of recommendations of 15th Finance Commission came into effect, whichever was 
earlier.
label: SERVICE_GAP

text:
7.5	
The Standing Finance Committee chaired by Secretary (DHR) in its meeting on 15.03.2021 
appraised the scheme and subsequently the competent authority approved it for continuation 
during the period of 15th Finance Commission (2021-22 to 2025-26) with the financial outlay of 
Rs. 192.36 crore for MRHRUs.
label: SERVICE_GAP

text:

MRHRU scheme is based on the experience of establishing such a unit at Ghatampur under 
the National Jalma Institute of Leprosy and other Mycobacterial Diseases (ICMR), Agra, where the 
methods of diagnosis and treatment as well as epidemiology were shown to be workable deep at 
the grass root rural settings.

9.1 Introduction (about the scheme and its objectives)
The Human Resource Development Scheme of Department of Health Research is intended to 
create a pool of talented health research personnel in the country by upgrading skills of faculty 
of Medical Colleges/Institutes, mid - career Scientists, medical students, etc., by specialized 
training in priority areas of health research in leading national and international institutions, 
encourage and support the trainees to develop and take up research projects for addressing 
critical national and local health problems and financial assistance to Institutions for up- gradation 
of infrastructure to enable such Institutions to provide training with state of the art technologies.
label: STAFF_GAP

text:
The scheme is approved during 15th Finance Commission Period (2021-22 
to 2025-26) and support under the program is imparted in following categories:
I. Short Term Fellowship for training in Foreign Institutes/Indian Institute:
Short Term Fellowship supports for training in Foreign/Indian Institutes in identified areas (1-3 
months) to persons employed as regular faculty those who are not above the age of 55 years.
label: STAFF_GAP

text:
During the year 2019-20, the programme was transferred to the ICMR 
for implementation.
label: SERVICE_GAP

text:
During 2021-22, fellowships for training in foreign institutes have been kept 
on hold during Covid-19 pandemic across the world.
label: STAFF_GAP

text:
Applications for training in indian institutes 
are invited every year through Department of Health Research.
label: STAFF_GAP

text:
In the year 2021-22 (upto 31st Dec 
2021), 2 fellowships have been supported under this programme.
label: STAFF_GAP

text:
Long Term Fellowships in India/abroad:
Long Term Fellowship supports for training abroad/Indian Institutions in identified priority areas 
(6-12 months) is meant for the persons employed as regular faculty and those who are not above 
the age of 45 years.
label: STAFF_GAP

text:
This component has also been transferred to 
the ICMR for implementation.
label: SERVICE_GAP

text:
During 2021-22, fellowships for training in foreign institutes have 
been kept on hold during Covid-19 pandemic across the world.
label: STAFF_GAP

text:
Applications for training in Indian 
Institutions are invited every year through Department of Health Research.
label: STAFF_GAP

text:
Start-up grant for fellows undergone long term/short term training supported by DHR:
The Start-up grant, with an average cost of Rs. 30 lakhs per research project, for three years, will 
be supported.
label: STAFF_GAP

CHAPTER
Health Technology Assessment in India 
(HTAIn)
10
Introduction
	
The Government of India is committed to extend healthcare services to its 1.38 billion 
population as part of India’s Universal Health Coverage (UHC) agenda.
label: STAFF_GAP

text:
The National Health Policy 
has recommended an increase in public spending on healthcare services from existing 1.15 percent 
to 2.5 percent of GDP.
label: SERVICE_GAP

text:
It notes that this can significantly reduce the out-of-pocket-expenditure of 
the overall healthcare spending.
label: COST_GAP

text:
Therefore, Department of Health Research has established a mechanism 
for Health Technology Assessment for evaluation of appropriateness and cost effectiveness of 
available and new health technologies in the country as part of research governance mandate of 
the Department.
label: COST_GAP

text:
Health Technology Assessment in India (HTAIn)
10.3	 Health Technology Assessment in India (HTAIn) is a sub-scheme under the umbrella scheme 
Human Resource and Capacity Building in the 15th Financial Commission approved for year 2021-22 
to 2025-26 under the Department of Health Research (DHR), Ministry of Health & Family Welfare 
(MoHFW), Government of India to facilitate the process of transparent and evidence-informed 
decision making in the field of healthcare.
label: STAFF_GAP

text:
medicines, devices and health programmes for its cost-effectiveness, 
clinical-effectiveness and equity issues by means of Health Technology Assessment (HTA), and in 
turn help in decision making for an efficient use of the limited health budget and provide people 
access to the quality health care reducing their out of pocket expenditures (OOPs) on health.
label: COST_GAP

text:
The National Health Policy, 2017 has also highlighted the importance of 
HTA by stating ‘One important capacity with respect to introduction of new technologies and their 
uptake into public health programmes is health technology assessment’.
label: STAFF_GAP

text:
10.5 Objectives and Significance of HTAIn
•	
To undertake HTA studies aiming at maximising health in the population, reducing out of 
pocket expenditure (OOP) and reducing inequity.
label: COST_GAP

text:
•	
To appraise health interventions and technologies based on available data on resource use, 
cost, clinical effectiveness, and safety.
label: COST_GAP

10.11	 Technical Partners are also institutes of the Central/ State Government identified with 
regards to their capacities, expertise and previous experience in the area of HTA/ Multi-centric 
research.
label: STAFF_GAP

text:
DHR provides funds to TPs on study to study basis, instead 
of fixed annual budget.
label: COST_GAP

text:
The outcome reports of the studies conducted by technical partners are 
submitted to the HTAIn Secretariat for approval from the TAC and Board.
label: QUALITY_GAP

text:
Stakeholders are individuals, organizations or communities that have a direct interest 
in the process and/or outcomes of the study under consideration by the HTAIn, therefore their 
participation is required to maintain the integrity of HTAIn and for a wider acceptance of its 
recommendations.
label: QUALITY_GAP

text:
Outcome reports are also uploaded on the website for 
the feedback and suggestions.
label: QUALITY_GAP

text:
The respective TP/ Resource Centres then submit a study proposal that contains the policy 
question(s), research question(s), objective(s), methodology, timeline, manpower required 
and the estimated budget.
label: COST_GAP

text:
After the appraisal and approval of the proposal by the TAC, TP/ Resource Centres conduct 
the HTA study and after completion of the study submit the Outcome Report to the Secretariat 
for TAC approval.
label: QUALITY_GAP

text:
Once the outcome report is approved by the TAC, it is submitted to the Board for final 
approval.
label: QUALITY_GAP

text:
The recommendations presented to the HTAIn Board for final approval and subsequently sent 
to the User Department for implementation.

text:
12.2	 From the beginning of the pandemic, Department of Health Research (DHR) was mandated 
with the responsibility of enhancing and facilitating COVID-19 testing, by ensuring suitable 
infrastructure and sufficient inventory for testing commodities across all States/UTs.
label: INFRA_GAP

text:
To manage and 
contain the surge, it was essential to increase the COVID-19 testing capacity of the country and 
this department proactively took a multitude of measures to further increasing the COVID-19 
testing capacity of the country in an equitable fashion along with ensuring availability of testing 
commodities.
label: STAFF_GAP

text:
12.3	 Department had onboarded almost 2300 labs by end of December 2020, amidst the spike of 
cases in early 2021, the department immediately took measures to ensure three shifts in all the 
testing labs to immediately meet the increased demand.
label: INFRA_GAP

text:
Through ICMR’s Memoranda of Understandings (MoUs), ICMR has partnered with 
leading universities from around the world to concentrate efforts on leading health issues such as 
cancer, diabetes, infectious diseases, and vaccine development.
label: INFRA_GAP

text:
These collaborations facilitate 
the exchange of scientific information, training, joint projects, and co-authorship of meetings, 
workshops, seminars, and symposia presentations.
label: STAFF_GAP

text:
The department coordinated with States/
UTs to ensure maximum capacity utilization of the existing testing labs and also on onboarding 
new testing labs to ICMR testing network.
label: INFRA_GAP

text:
ICMR onboarded an additional 800 labs to its testing 
network in the year 2021.
label: INFRA_GAP

text:
DHR supported in establishing 26 high through-put laboratories across 
the country with capacity to perform more than 3000 tests per day for increasing the testing 
capacity for infectious diseases.
label: STAFF_GAP

text:
These machines supported in increasing the 
testing capacity of existing labs.
label: INFRA_GAP

text:
DHR also coordinated setting up of 16 biosafety laboratories in 
various states through the prestigious PM cares fund.
label: COST_GAP

text:
12.4	 Additionally, in areas were testing facility were minimal or over utilized, the Department 
and ICMR coordinated with private players to bring in mobile testing laboratories for COIVD-19 
testing and the first of such laboratories were launched by ICMR/DHR along with Spice Health in 
Delhi.
label: INFRA_GAP

text:
Newer testing technologies were promoted by ICMR /DHR and timely guidelines were issued to 
promote their successful implementation and put them to use.
label: SERVICE_GAP

text:
DHR coordinated with all the manufacturers and organized stakeholders consultations to identify 
their issues and support them with the requisite regulatory, logistical and facilitation support 
needed to enhance manufacturing capacity provided instrumental in ensuring unhindered 
supplies to the States/UTs.
label: STAFF_GAP

text:
DHR supported the manufacturers with expediting their regulatory 
approvals, supported them for uninterrupted logistics, local movement permissions, resolution 
of operational constraints faced by them, during lock downs by coordinating with respective 
district administrations to ensure uninterrupted production and availability of COVID-19 testing 
commodities.
label: ACCESS_GAP

text:
Additionally, the validation 
centers handhold the  domestic players in ensuring development of quality products and thus to 
accomplish self-reliance in production of COVID-19 testing commodities in line with the mission 
of Atma-nirbhar Bharat.
12.7	 Support was also provided by expediting customs clearance of imported raw materials of 
COVID-19 diagnostic kits and also by reducing the customs duty on COVID-19 diagnostic commodities 
for a specific time interval with support from the relevant Departments in Government of India.
label: INFRA_GAP

text:
Currently, the country has a capacity to produce more than 80 lakhs for RAT kits and RT-PCR kits 
Mapping view of the National Testing Laboratory Network

Department of Health Research
109
per day and this makes India, a country with surplus capacity of COVID-19 diagnostics with huge 
provisions for export.
label: STAFF_GAP

text:
Thus, all 
these initiatives made India free of import dependency and also a country with surplus production 
capacity thereby proudly demonstrating the strength of Atma-nirbhar Bharat.
label: STAFF_GAP

text:
Atma-nirbhar Bharat 
initiative also created a healthy competition in the diagnostic market resulting in huge drop in the 
cost of covid-19 diagnostic commodities.
label: SERVICE_GAP

visibility and access of these products to the States/UTs were ensured by onboarding of all these 
manufactures in Government e-

14
	
Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR), New Delhi, is the apex body in India for the 
formulation, coordination and promotion of biomedical research and is one of the oldest medical 
research bodies in the world.
label: INFRA_GAP

text:
The ICMR is funded by the Government of India through the 
Department of Health Research, Ministry of Health & Family Welfare.
label: COST_GAP

text:
14.3	 The Council’s research priorities coincide with the National health priorities such as control 
and management of communicable diseases, fertility control, maternal and child health, control 
of nutritional disorders, developing alternative strategies for health care delivery, containment 
within safety limits of environmental and occupational health problems; research on major 
non-communicable diseases like cancer, cardiovascular diseases, blindness, diabetes and other 
metabolic and haematological disorders; mental health and drug research (including traditional 
remedies).
label: QUALITY_GAP

text:
14.4	 ICMR has also demonstrated its commitment to the future of medical research through 
its professional development training and capacity building.
label: STAFF_GAP

text:
This includes training programs, 
workshops, and short-term research studentships for those preparing for a career in medicine and 
medical research.
label: STAFF_GAP

text:
It also includes research fellowships and short-term visiting fellowships for up 
and coming researchers to expand their skills and knowledge early in their career.
label: STAFF_GAP

Drone-based delivery of COVID-19 Vaccine: Union Minister for Health & Family Welfare, 
Shri Mansukh Mandaviya, launched  ICMR’s Drone Response and Outreach in North East 
(i-Drone).i-Drone is another pioneering initiative undertaken by ICMR to transform India’s 
health ecosystem.
label: SERVICE_GAP

text:
First sortie with vaccines was conducted from Bishnupur District 
Hospital, to PHC Karang today.
label: INFRA_GAP

text:
It takes approx 2.5 hrs (25 
Km by road, 3 Km by boat and further 2 Km by trek) to reach PHC Karang from District 
Hospital Bishnupur.
label: INFRA_GAP

text:
Whereas the drone took only 15 minutes to reach the PHC Karang 
from Bishnupur District hospital.
label: INFRA_GAP

text:
This is first such initiative of vaccine delivery through 
drone from plain to island in south-east Asia.
label: SERVICE_GAP

text:

Malaria Elimination Research Alliance (MERA) India: ICMR funded 32 projects under 
MERA India initiative, including eight individual studies, and 24 multi-centric projects 
under Task Force mode on four themes viz low-density infection detection (LDI); vector 
bionomics, Geographical Information System and community behavior.
label: SERVICE_GAP

text:
The salient features 
of the MERA-India multi-centric projects include mentoring by the experts; standard 
common objectives and methodology to maintain research quality and uniformity of 
data generation; and extensive peer-review.
label: QUALITY_GAP

order to provide training to the young investigators, MERA-India organized workshops at 
ICMR-NIMR in the multi-centric project themes.

(STS)-2020 Program.

2016 and continued taking treatment underwent viral load testing and over 90% showed 
that the virus in their blood was adequately suppressed.
